gauge.name: Dyberry Creek near Jadwin Reservoir

impacts.statement: The level at which the reservoir begins to spill. The emergency spillway is an ungated, excavated channel located along the reservoir rim. Discharge through the emergency spillway is uncontrolled and intended to prevent overtopping and catastrophic failure of the dam during a high water event. Significant flooding can occur during spillway flow. Please pay attention to messaging from your local authorities if water is observed flowing through the spillway.

dataAttribution.text: USGS--Water Resources of the United States
dataAttribution.title: US Geological Survey
Observations courtesy of U.S. Geological Survey

impactsLowWaters.impact: Reservoir is normally dry, used for flood control purposes only. Pool elevation readings below 990.00 ft (NGVD) do not represent storage in the reservoir and indicate water levels in the stilling well only. Pool elevations below 990.00 ft (NGVD) indicate that the reservoir is dry.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are not available. Only observed stages are available for this point.
